AU.meaning: Programming is intended for younger children 2–11; commercial stations must show at least 30 minutes of P-rated content each weekday and weekends at all times. No advertisements may be shown during P-rated programs.

BR.meaning: Content suitable for viewers over the age of 14.

CA.meaning: Shows which are exempt from ratings (such as news and sports programming) will not display an on-screen rating at all.

CA-QC.meaning: Only to be viewed by adults and may contain extreme violence and graphic sexual content. It is mostly used for 18+ movies and pornography.

DE.meaning: Can be aired at any time.

GB.meaning: The U symbol stands for Universal. A U film should be suitable for audiences aged four years and over.

KR.meaning: This rating is only for knowledge based game shows; lifestyle shows; documentary shows; news; current topic discussion shows; education/culture shows; sports that excludes MMA or other violent sports; and other programs that Korea Communications Standards Commission recognizes.

LT.meaning: Intended for adult viewers from the age of 18 (corresponding to the age-appropriate index N-18) and broadcast between 23 (11pm) and 6 (6am) hours; Limited to minors and intended for adult audiences.

PT.meaning: Acompanhamento Parental (may not be suitable for children under 12, parental guidance advised).

RU.meaning: Only teens the age of 16 or older can watch.

TH.meaning: Sor - Educational movies which the public should be encouraged to see.

US.meaning: This program is specifically designed to be viewed by adults and therefore may be unsuitable for children under 17.

MY.meaning: No age limit. Can be broadcast anytime.

BG.meaning: Can be viewed for each age.

MX.meaning: Aimed at children (can be broadcast anytime).

ID.meaning: Suitable for general audiences over the age of 2 years.

TR.meaning: General audience. Suitable for all ages.

PL.meaning: Positive or neutral view of the world, little to no violence, non-sexual love, and no sexual content.

IL.meaning: Exempt from classification. This rating is usually applied to live broadcasts.

TW.meaning: Suitable for watching by general audiences.

ZA.meaning: This is a programme/film that does not contain any obscenity, and is suitable for family viewing. A logo must be displayed in the corner of the screen for 30 seconds after each commercial break.
